pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 66 creates file at this path using cat >
setup_task.sh line 11 creates file at this path
setup_task.sh line 7 creates directory; export_result.sh line 8 defines path; task description line 7 specifies SVG export requirement
Task description requires 'Create a SECOND PAGE named Token Refresh Flow'; spec has Page-1 (initial) + new Token Refresh Flow page
Setup creates initial diagram with 3 lifelines (User, Client Application, Authorization Server). Spec requires adding Token Store and Resource Server (participants 4-5 in spec). Total: 3 + 2 = 5

Initial diagram has 6 messages (M1-M6). Token Exchange phase adds 4 messages (7-10). Resource Access phase adds 4 main messages (11-14) with message 14b as alt variant. Total: 6 + 4 + 4 = 14 (not counting alt variant separately)

All metadata terms verified present in oauth2_sequence_spec.txt: Resource Server (participant 5), Token Store (participant 4), refresh_token (message 10), Bearer (message 11), alt (line 41 combined fragment)

pi_items.metadata_value: [Resource Server, Token Store, refresh_token, Bearer, alt]
pi_items.source: Metadata lists one set of required_terms but export_result.sh line 138 uses different terms in analyzer. Both sets are present in spec but verifier script does not check for metadata terms.

privileged_info_summary: The OAuth 2.0 sequence diagram task is fully synthetic, with all data generated by setup_task.sh. The task requires completing a Draw.io diagram with OAuth 2.0 protocol flows: initial auth (6 messages), token exchange (4 messages), and resource access (4 messages) = 14 total messages on page 1 with 5 lifelines (User, Client Application, Authorization Server, Token Store, Resource Server). A second page models the token refresh flow. The specification is based on RFC 6749. All metadata file paths, page count, lifeline count, and message count have been verified as accurate. However, there is a critical discrepancy: the metadata declares required_terms as [Resource Server, Token Store, refresh_token, Bearer, alt], but the actual verifier script in export_result.sh checks for [access_token, refresh_token, bearer, authorization_code, 401, introspect] - a completely different set of terms, though both sets appear in the specification document.
